rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDWARD COLE WILSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/13/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Still no proof of service of the notice of this hearing has been filed. (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) The only I&A filed in this matter (on 5/9/22) showed the estate appraised at $1,174,257.89, not $1,103,178.07, as alleged in paragraph 10 of the petition. (4) There was no Corrected I&A filed in these proceedings. (5) If an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting, not just photocopies. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (6) The copy of the brokerage account statement attached to the petition does not show that it is an ESTATE account. (7) The accounting is missing the assets on hand at the beginning of the accounting period. [Probate Code Section 1061(a)(1).] (8) Why is the disability check still on hand? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/2/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE AKIKO SPINDLE 2021 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/14/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: It is common practice for an informational I&A to be filed for a substituted judgment trust or for a receipt to be filed in the related conservatorship case; there is no rule or requirement, per se, but practitioners do so in order to confirm the starting assets in the trust accounting. It is now clear that the trust was not funded until after the end of the conservator's last (third) accounting, so there is a gap between 11/30/21, the ending date of the conservator's accounting, and the date when assets were actually transferred to this trust. In other words, the accounting begins with assets that were NOT on hand as of 6/10/21, and those amounts cannot be verified until the conservator's accounting for the period beyond 11/30/21 is filed. In order to coordinate the accountings in both cases (and synchronize with the biennial CIU report), this one should be continued to next February and amended again to incorporate a later ending date. Alternatively, a second amended accounting could be filed now to show NO assets on hand as of 6/10/21 and assets received during this accounting period (Additional Property Received During Period of Account), supported by a receipt filed in the conservatorship case. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/2/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (NB: This is not an interim accounting; it is the trustee's first accounting. A supplement or amendment to a petition should stand on its own and not "answer" issues posted in a tentative ruling. Especially where defective notice needs to be corrected, a proof of service of the correct notice should simply be filed without explanation or narration in a pleading.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAI EDUARDO PEREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/23/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] Courtesy copies of the supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the petitioner, and a proof of service of the complete notice must be received by the assigned Examiner by 5/12/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
